title: Uttarakhand Weather: Rain and snowfall brought relief in hilly districts, heatwave intensifies in plains including Dehradun - news9live.com

Uttarakhand Weather: Rain and snowfall brought relief in hilly districts, heatwave intensifies in plains including Dehradun
Uttarakhand’s weather is showing two extremes today. While rain and snowfall have brought relief in the hilly districts, the plains including Dehradun are facing severe heat. The Meteorological Department says this pattern may continue until April 5, keeping conditions unpredictable across the state.
Dehradun: The weather in Uttarakhand is currently divided into two contrasting conditions. On one side, the hilly districts are enjoying cool and pleasant weather due to light rain and snowfall, while on the other side, the plains are suffering under the scorching sun and rising temperatures.
According to the Meteorological Centre, light rain is expected in several hilly districts today. Areas above 3,500 meters may even witness snowfall. This has kept the atmosphere cool and pleasant in the mountains, giving residents and tourists much-needed relief from heat. Continuous showers and snowfall have helped control temperatures, making the weather enjoyable in places like Chamoli, Uttarkashi, and Pithoragarh.
Heatwave in the Plains
In contrast, the plains including Dehradun, Haridwar, and Udham Singh Nagar are facing severe heat. The sun has been shining brightly since morning, and the dry weather has made conditions worse. The temperature is steadily rising, making it difficult for people to step out during the day.
Residents of Dehradun said that while recent rains had provided some relief, the heat has returned with greater intensity. The afternoons are particularly harsh, forcing people to stay indoors.
Unpredictable Weather Until April 5
The Meteorological Department has predicted that this dual weather pattern will continue until April 5. While the mountains will remain cloudy with chances of rain and snowfall, the plains are likely to experience increasing heat and dry conditions. This unpredictability has made it challenging for people to plan their daily activities.
Impact on Daily Life
In the plains, the rising heat is affecting daily routines. People are avoiding outdoor work during the afternoon hours, and markets are seeing fewer visitors. Meanwhile, in the hills, the pleasant weather has encouraged tourism, with visitors enjoying the cool climate and scenic beauty.
Farmers in the plains are also concerned about the rising temperatures, which could affect crops if the heatwave continues. On the other hand, rainfall in the hills is seen as beneficial for agriculture and water availability.
Uttarakhand’s weather on April 1 reflects the state’s diverse climate. While the hills are enjoying cool relief with rain and snowfall, the plains are struggling with severe heat. With the Meteorological Department predicting this pattern to continue until April 5, residents across the state must prepare for both extremes.
